content: Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup. Date 2026-02-03. I keep trying note systems and they never stick. Quick history: Evernote 2016–2019, Roam trial 2020, Notion heavy setup 2021, Obsidian experiments 2022, plain Markdown + git in 2023. Pattern: initial enthusiasm, 1–3 weeks of tidy input, then friction (mobile capture slow, too many templates, tags explode), backlog grows, I stop doing the weekly review. Concrete numbers: right now my Inbox is ~482 notes (mostly meeting bullets, half-baked ideas, recipes, family todo), I did a weekly review only 4 times in 2025, average processing time per note >5 minutes because I over-structure everything. My real goals: capture ideas quickly, keep project next-actions visible, searchable code snippets, meeting notes that link to tasks, and a small reference for family finances/taxes/recipes. What I suspect: failure is mostly because I didn't commit to a single capture point, I tried to make the system do too many things (GTD + PARA + Zettelkasten all at once), and reviews are vague. I want a very small experiment: a 30-day protocol starting 2026-02-10 (so 2026-02-10 to 2026-03-11). I want rules I can actually follow and measurable goals. Help me plan the 30-day experiment and point out blind spots.
Reflection: you need one capture, strict processing rules, and measurable weekly targets. Sharp question: which single capture tool will you commit to for the whole 30 days?

I'll commit to Obsidian as the single capture tool (desktop + mobile with Obsidian Sync). Concrete rules for the 30 days: 1) Single Inbox vault/folder in Obsidian for capture. 2) One idea per note. 3) Title format: YYYYMMDD - short-topic. 4) Tags limited to #inbox, #project, #evergreen, #reference; add project-specific tag only when promoting to Projects. 5) Templater/new-note shortcut that adds frontmatter (created, capture-type). 6) Processing cadence: nightly quick triage (1–2 minutes) and full weekly review Sunday 30 minutes. Targets: process Inbox to zero each Sunday, processed/week <=20, daily capture <2 minutes. Start date 2026-02-10, end 2026-03-11. I want a minimal daily ritual — suggest it.
What's the single smallest daily ritual you'll actually do that keeps momentum (exact steps and max time)?

Daily ritual I can commit to: 1) On phone, quick-capture any idea into Obsidian Inbox using shortcut (max 90 seconds). 2) At night, 1-minute triage: mark note as #reference if passive, convert to project stub if it has a next-action, or delete if garbage. Rule: if next action <2 minutes, do it immediately; if >2 minutes, add to Projects.md with a single next action and deadline. Projects.md lives at root and is the source of truth. For weekly review (Sunday 30 minutes): process Inbox to zero, move items to Projects.md or to a Reference folder, convert worthwhile notes into evergreen notes and link them. I also want an automation: surface unlinked evergreen notes older than 14 days. Plan: start 2026-02-10 with an initial cleanup session of 90 minutes to reduce backlog to <200 (target) before starting stricter routine. Can you suggest a minimal automation or query to surface unlinked notes older than 14 days?

Use an Obsidian search query for unlinked notes: a plugin query that finds notes with no outgoing/ backlink count = 0 and created:<-14d. Sharp question: do you prefer a plugin-based query (Community Plugins) or a plain Dataview search?

I'll use Community Plugins — Dataview + Templater already. So Dataview query for unlinked notes older than 14 days is fine. Operational decisions: 1) Calendar reminder — Google Calendar recurring event Mondays 09:00 named 'Notes Review' (30 min). 2) Obsidian quick-capture shortcut on the phone home screen for instant capture. 3) Prune system to 3 spaces: Inbox, Projects (Projects.md + project notes), Reference/Evergreen. 4) Decision rule recap: do-it-now if <2 minutes, delete if clearly irrelevant, promote to project with one next action if it needs work, evergreen only if it can be linked to an existing note or future project. Metrics to track weekly: captured, processed, avg time per note, Inbox count, links created, weekly-review done (Y/N), projects with next-actions. Targets: processed >= captured, processed <=20/week, Inbox <=5 by week 3, links/week >=5, weekly reviews 4/4. Post-mortem scheduled for 2026-03-12. Can you help me by tracking/ prompting these metrics weekly and prepare a short post-mortem template on 2026-03-12?
I can prompt weekly and prepare the post-mortem template. Quick question: would you like weekly prompts via calendar notifications or via a one-line checklist note in Obsidian created every Monday?

Use both: calendar notification as the hard reminder, and create a weekly checklist note in Obsidian automatically via Templater on Mondays named 'Weekly Review YYYY-WW' with the metrics table. Concrete logging fields per week: Week start date, captured (n), processed (n), avg time/note (min), Inbox count (n), links created (n), reviews completed (Y/N), projects with next-actions (n), time spent (min). Targets repeated: processed >= captured, processed <=20, Inbox <=5 by week 3, links >=5/week, reviews 4/4. If by 2026-03-11 targets are met for at least 3 metrics, continue; else revert to simpler capture-only routine. Remind me on 2026-03-12 to run the post-mortem and generate the template there (bulleted prompts: what worked, what failed, numbers, decision). Also I will run the 90-minute initial cleanup on 2026-02-09 to hit Inbox <200 before the start date. Can you confirm you'll remind on 2026-03-12 and prepare the post-mortem template then?

Confirmed: I'll remind you on 2026-03-12 and prepare a post-mortem template. Final quick question: for the weekly metrics note, do you want the numbers entered manually or an automated script that pulls created/modified dates and backlink counts?
